page: Microsoft Azure E192ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server type is designed for high-capacity memory-intensive workloads. The virtual machine does not include integrated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The server features 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. For local data storage, the instance is equipped with 11339 GB of local disk capacity and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. This combination of high-capacity RAM and substantial local storage supports large-scale data processing without relying solely on external network-attached storage.

From a resource density perspective, the E192ds_v7 offers a high concentration of memory and local storage per virtual machine instance. This density allows for consolidation of large workloads, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for hardware-accelerated machine learning or rendering tasks. The qualitative cost profile reflects its position as a high-tier, memory-optimized resource designed for enterprise-scale deployments.

Appropriate workloads for this configuration include large relational databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-warehousing tasks that require substantial memory capacity alongside fast local storage access.
description: Microsoft Azure E192ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 192 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 1536.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Storage capabilities include 11339 GB of local disk space alongside Premium Storage compatibility, though the instance lacks integrated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high density of memory and local storage makes this server type highly efficient for consolidating massive data footprints. It is structurally optimized for workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data-warehousing operations.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 192 dedicated cores, 1.5 TB of RAM, and extensive local storage capacity.
